steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has two moves. First: analyses call The Night Watch an “exemplar of the Baroque-era style.” Second: art historians challenge the existence of a distinct Baroque era and claim the Baroque aesthetic was just an extension of Renaissance style. The blank goes inside the historians' claim — it should signal they are asserting the actual truth of their position, not just contrasting or adding to it.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — in reality,: Signals that what follows is the true state of affairs — the historians assert the Baroque era is actually just Renaissance continuation. Fits perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — first of all,: Introduces a list or sequence; nothing in the passage signals more points are coming. ✗
  • Choice C — similarly,: Signals similarity to a previous idea, but the historians' view opposes the characterization in the first sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — nevertheless,: Signals a contrast within the historians' own claim, which isn't needed — their claim is a single direct assertion. ✗
That is the correct answer.
